commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ralph-wiggum-v3.3 (#21)

## Overview

This PR revamps `ralph interview` to replace the existing "dump all
questions at once, type a free-form essay" interaction with a guided,
one-question-at-a-time flow featuring pre-generated answer suggestions.
Claude's question-generation phase now outputs structured JSON
containing each question and 2–3 suggested answer options. The CLI
presents each question individually with an arrow-key selection menu
(TTY) or numbered list (non-TTY), and the selected answers are passed
back to Claude as a structured JSON array instead of a free-form text
block.

## Key Changes

- **`templates/questions.md`** — Modified in-place to instruct Claude to
output structured JSON (`{"questions": [{id, question, options[]}]}`)
instead of a plain-text numbered list. Retains the same
`{{PROJECT_NAME}}`, `{{ROUND_NUM}}`, `{{TOTAL_ROUNDS}}` template
variables.
- **`templates/generate_tasks.md`** — Updated to accept a single
`{{QA_JSON}}` variable (a JSON array of `{question, answer}` pairs)
replacing the separate `{{QUESTIONS}}` and `{{ANSWERS}}` blocks.
- **`ralph/parse.py`** — `parse_generate_tasks_md()` updated to accept a
single `qa_json: str` parameter. Internal helper renamed from
`_resolve_questions_block()` to `_resolve_qa_block()`. `CommentCommand`
and `EnrichCommand` call sites (using `user_comment=`) are unaffected.
- **`ralph/run.py`** — Four changes:
- Added `_parse_questions_json(raw: str) -> list[dict] | None` to parse
Claude's JSON output, with markdown fence stripping and graceful
fallback on parse failure.
- Extracted `_open_multiline_editor(preamble: str) -> str` shared helper
from `_collect_user_answers()`. TTY path uses `prompt_toolkit`; non-TTY
path reads lines until a `.` sentinel to avoid exhausting stdin across
multiple questions.
- Added `_collect_guided_answers(questions: list[dict]) -> str`
implementing the one-at-a-time selection UX. TTY mode uses
`prompt_toolkit.application.Application` with `FormattedTextControl` and
arrow-key navigation; non-TTY mode uses a numbered list with
`sys.stdin.readline()` and re-prompts on invalid input.
- Updated `run_interview_loop()` to use the guided path when structured
JSON is successfully parsed, with automatic fallback to the legacy
free-form path when parsing fails.
- **`ralph/commands.py`** — `InterviewCommand.execute()` updated so
`make_amend_prompts` callables accept a single `qa_json: str` argument
instead of separate `(questions, answers)` parameters.
- **`tests/test_cmd_interview.py`** — Replaced with comprehensive tests
for all new behaviour: 7 tests for `_parse_questions_json`, 4 for
`_open_multiline_editor`, 8 for `_collect_guided_answers`, and 7 for
`run_interview_loop` (guided and fallback branches). All pre-existing
command-level tests retained. Total: 38 tests passing.

## Testing

All 38 tests in `tests/test_cmd_interview.py` pass. Test coverage
includes:

- `_parse_questions_json`: valid JSON string, JSON wrapped in markdown
fences, malformed JSON (returns `None`), empty questions array,
whitespace-only input, missing `questions` key.
- `_open_multiline_editor`: TTY path (preamble output, return value
passed through via mocked `prompt_toolkit` session); non-TTY path (reads
lines until sentinel without exhausting stdin).
- `_collect_guided_answers`: valid in-range selection, out-of-range
input followed by valid selection (re-prompt), non-integer input
(re-prompt), "Describe yourself..." selection, Ctrl+C exit
(`sys.exit(0)`), Ctrl+C message, JSON string output format validation,
single-question case.
- `run_interview_loop`: guided path (structured questions parsed →
`_collect_guided_answers` called → `make_amend_prompts[i]` called with
returned JSON string), fallback path (parse returns `None` →
`_collect_user_answers` called → result wrapped in JSON → fallback
warning printed), fallback on empty list, `_collect_guided_answers` not
called on fallback, multiple rounds.

## Notes

- No new dependencies were introduced. Arrow-key navigation uses
`prompt_toolkit`, which was already an installed dependency.
- The non-TTY multiline editor uses a `.` sentinel (a single period on
its own line) to delimit the end of a free-form answer. This allows
multiple "Describe yourself..." answers in a single interview round
without exhausting stdin.
- `questionary` and `InquirerPy` were intentionally avoided to prevent
adding new dependencies.
- `ralph comment`, `ralph enrich`, `ralph execute`, and all other
commands are unaffected by this change.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Enforce strongest possible duo on match 1 per rotation

Remove the equal-sum pairing exception so only pairings that
maximize the top duo sum are kept, ensuring the strongest duo
always plays match 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 10 bugs found in code audit

- form_finder: search filter "s"/"q" matched any field containing the letter, skipping legitimate fields like address/message
- gemini/opus: greedy JSON regex captured too much text, now non-greedy
- gemini: dir() checked module scope instead of locals() for error variable
- database: connection leak on exceptions - added try/finally to update_site and add_submission
- main: asyncio.shield on already-cancelled task replaced with direct await
- captcha_solver: duplicate "+" operator in math captcha solver
- settings.html: f.value.toLowerCase() crashed on null toggle values
- start.sh: SSL setup failure aborted entire startup due to set -e
- bot_manager: log file descriptor and PID file leak on natural bot exit
- config_manager: .env values with quotes were read with literal quote characters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Router: add missing Messages import (v0.2.193)

Router.py referenced Messages.MSG_ROUTER_FORBIDDEN and other message
constants without importing the Messages module, causing NameError on
forbidden routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add transparent auto-login for ZUEB authenticated commands

When a session is missing or a token expires mid-flight, the system
now automatically re-authenticates using ZUEB_USERNAME / ZUEB_PASSWORD
from the environment, making nightly scheduler and other unattended
flows resilient to token expiry without user intervention.

- Add _auto_login / _ensure_session / _is_sso_error / _run_with_relogin
  helpers to bot/agent/helper.py
- Refactor schedule, attendance, attendance-punch to use the new helpers
  (L2: auto-login on missing session; L3: retry once on SSO failure)
- Make login command args optional with env-var fallback
- /login handler in commands.py supports no-arg env-var login;
  message deletion skipped when no password was in the message
- Update system prompt rule 4: agent goes directly to skills instead
  of prompting for /login first
- zueb-login SKILL.md: add no-arg call pattern

The middleware www→non-www redirect was conflicting with Vercel's own
domain redirect, causing an infinite redirect loop. The www→non-www
redirect should be configured in Vercel's domain settings instead.

commit.message:
fix(review): focus restore via useEffect + setTimeout post-animation (Guardrail #30)

onCloseAutoFocus alone doesn't work — Radix's 200ms exit animation
cleanup overrides the focus we set. Switch to useEffect watching
open state + setTimeout(250ms) to restore focus AFTER animation ends.
onCloseAutoFocus still preventDefault() to block Radix default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(site): use generic constraint instead of union for localized() params

The `Record<string, unknown> | object` union from the previous fix still
fails in CI — TypeScript resolves against Record<string, unknown> first
and rejects typed interfaces lacking an index signature. Using
`<T extends object>` accepts any object type cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

互連改按鈕樣式、加贊助按鈕、刪舊導流按鈕

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: respawn dead agents for gate-retry instead of skipping

Pi agents exit after completing work, so gate-retry found them dead and
gave up. Now respawns a fresh agent process in the existing worktree
when the original is dead, allowing the fix prompt to be sent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve Linux support and error handling for services

- Add support for php-fpm (static-php) alongside php-cgi (Windows)
- Generate php-fpm.conf for Linux/macOS static-php builds
- Improve binary detection to prioritize direct php-fpm path
- Add helpful error message when wrong-platform binaries detected
- Propagate initialization errors properly instead of ignoring them
- Return service statuses even on failure so UI shows error state
- Show alert dialogs to user when service start/stop/restart fails
- Simplify php.ini to avoid extension warnings with static-php

mastery + multi-agent observability, promptfoo for LLM eval/red-teaming,
